title: Correlated subquery with parent_as/1
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.users_with_recent_post/1` that takes `days` and returns users whose most recent post was within the last `days` days. Use a correlated subquery with `parent_as(:user)`: the outer query is `from u in User, as: :user`, and the subquery uses `parent_as(:user).id` to correlate. Use `exists(subquery(q))` to filter. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: `parent_as` is a non-obvious Ecto API that Claude rarely reaches for. Must combine it with `exists` and `subquery`.
research_citation: Ecto aggregates-and-subqueries hexdocs: 'from c in Comment, where: parent_as(:posts).id == c.post_id'
